name: Priya Sharma
description: Senior engineer / tech lead; UK tax resident, Indian NRI. NRE account (tax-free interest in UK under DTAA), NRO account (interest taxable in UK above PSA), EPF (Employees' Provident Fund, India), Indian equity mutual funds (LTCG/STCG under Indian rules + UK treatment as foreign income). 2021 snapshot age 33: smaller UK portfolio, active Indian investments. 2026 snapshot age 38: expanded UK wealth, DTAA relief claimed, LRS remittance tracking. Tests: cross-border wrapper detection, DTAA credit, Indian MF tax treatment, NRE vs NRO income handling, RBI LRS tracker.

jurisdiction.note: NRI status maintained. UK statutory residency test: automatic UK resident (183 days+ in UK). India: non-resident per FEMA/IT Act for financial purposes.

cross_border.note_2021: In 2021 UK deemed domicile required 17 of 20 UK tax years (old rules). Priya has 8 years — not yet deemed domiciled. FIG (Foreign Income and Gains) regime not applicable in 2021 — arose from April 2025.

lrs_remittance_fy_2021.purpose: family maintenance + Indian mutual fund investment

indian_assets.uk_tax_treatment: Interest EXEMPT in UK under DTAA Art 11(3) — NRE account interest specifically exempted in India-UK treaty. Must claim exemption on SA return.
indian_assets.note: NRE account: Indian tax-free interest AND UK DTAA exemption. Most efficient Indian cash holding for NRI.

indian_assets.uk_tax_treatment: Interest taxable in UK at marginal rate. Credit for Indian TDS paid (30% in India; UK reduces to treaty rate). PSA £1,000 applies first.

EPF interest: taxable as savings income in UK if NRI and UK tax resident. DTAA may reduce double taxation — treaty analysis needed. Maturity: tax-free in India under sec 10(12) EEA. UK may tax growth accrued during UK residency.
indian_assets.nri_note: As NRI, cannot contribute further to EPF. Balance grows at Indian declared rate. Withdrawal possible on 2-month non-employment notice.

indian_assets.uk_tax_treatment: Gains taxable in UK as CGT on disposal. Indian tax paid is creditable. Non-reporting fund for UK purposes — gains likely taxable as INCOME not CGT unless reporting status obtained. Must check HMRC reporting fund status for each fund.
indian_assets.uk_cgt_alert: Non-reporting Indian MF: gains taxed as income (not CGT) in UK. Material adverse treatment for HR taxpayer.

indian_assets.uk_tax_treatment: PPF interest: Indian tax-exempt under Sec 80C. UK position unclear — HMRC has not issued definitive guidance on PPF. Conservative position: taxable as savings income in UK above PSA. Treaty credit if Indian tax applied.
indian_assets.nri_note: NRIs cannot open new PPF accounts. Existing accounts: can continue until maturity; cannot extend beyond 15-year term.

streams.note: DTAA exemption — not taxable in UK

engine_notes: NRE interest: DTAA Art 11(3) exemption. Engine must detect NRE wrapper and apply nil UK tax.
NRO interest: taxable in UK; Indian TDS 30% creditable to UK rate (higher rate = 40%). Net UK tax if HR = 40% - 30% = 10% additional.
Indian MF: non-reporting fund — gains taxable as INCOME in UK (not CGT). In 2021 this means income tax at 40%.
EPF: continuing growth taxable as savings income in UK above PSA (£1,000 for HR taxpayer = £500).
CGT AEA £12,300 in 2021 — partially used (£4,800 realised). Still £7,500 remaining.
Deemed domicile NOT triggered: 8 years, not 17. Remittance basis potentially available (charge applies from year 8). Priya uses arising basis — simpler.
LRS remittance tracking: RBI limit $250k/year. £50k remitted outward — well within limit.

expected_output_envelope.confidence_notes: Medium confidence: Indian MF reporting status uncertain; PPF UK treatment requires HMRC guidance; EPF repatriation cost unknown.
expected_output_envelope.coi_notes: Non-reporting Indian MF: gains taxed as income vs CGT — material drag. NRO restructuring opportunity.

jurisdiction.note: UK deemed domicile: 13 years in 2026. Still below 15-of-20 test for IHT. Pre-April 2025 rules: 17 years. New rules from April 2025: 10-year IHT test. Priya now has 13 years — EXCEEDS new 10-year IHT threshold. Full worldwide IHT exposure from April 2025.

cross_border.iht_deemed_domicile_note: From April 2025, new 10-year residence test for IHT. Priya has 13 years UK residence → worldwide IHT exposure. Indian assets now IN scope of UK IHT.

lrs_remittance_fy_2026.purpose: EPF partial withdrawal + Indian MF redemption

pensions.note: Legacy DC pot — consolidation into SIPP pending.

indian_assets.uk_tax_treatment: DTAA exemption applied — NRE interest claimed exempt on 2025/26 SA return. Confirmed with UK tax adviser.
Taxable in UK. DTAA credit for TDS reduces liability. NRO balance reduced — repatriating to ISA via LRS.

EPF interest: HMRC issued guidance 2024 — EPF treated as registered pension-equivalent for UK purposes IF contributions made pre-UK residency. Priya joined EPF in India 2010-2017 before UK arrival (2013). Mixed treatment: pre-2013 accretion potentially exempt; post-2013 accrual taxable.
indian_assets.iht_note: From April 2025, Priya is IHT-deemed-domicile. EPF now IN UK IHT estate.

indian_assets.uk_tax_treatment: Partially converted to UK reporting fund status via adviser. UK CGT applies on disposal; Indian tax credited.
indian_assets.uk_cgt_note: Reporting status obtained — gains now CGT not income. Significant improvement vs 2021. CGT AEA only £3k in 2026.

indian_assets.uk_tax_treatment: PPF: HMRC clarified 2024 — PPF treated as exempt savings account for UK purposes (similar to ISA) where contributions made before UK arrival. Priya's PPF opened 2008 — pre-UK arrival. Interest EXEMPT from UK tax per extra-statutory concession.

engine_notes: IHT: deemed domicile from April 2025 (10-yr rule). Indian assets NOW in UK IHT estate. Engine must detect this threshold crossing.
EPF: mixed pre/post-UK arrival treatment. Conservative: all taxable in UK above PSA.
Indian MF: reporting fund status obtained — CGT treatment, not income. CGT AEA only £3k (2026 rate).
NRE: DTAA exemption still applies regardless of deemed domicile status.
SIPP enters estate April 2027 — 354 days. Engine must flag deadline.
Higher rate taxpayer: income £108k + NRO interest. PA taper starts at £100k. Adjusted net income £108k - £11,400 (pension) = £96,600. Below £100k — PA retained.
CGT realised £2,800 (within £3,000 AEA). No additional CGT liability.

expected_output_envelope.confidence_notes: Medium: Indian assets cross-border treatment complex; EPF historical split needs specialist verification.
expected_output_envelope.iht_notes: Estate ~£380k. NRB £325k. Modest IHT exposure on Indian assets above NRB now that deemed-domicile triggered.
expected_output_envelope.coi_notes: Indian MF reporting status now correct — main CoI was remediated. Remaining CoI: life cover not in trust; DC legacy pot unconsolidated.
